events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need produce exactly one valid JSON object per schema, no markdown code blocks. Need assess scenario: thread locker color misapplied (blue vs red - ID). Setting manufacturing assembly floor, rough-complete. Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Work is non-compliant: wrong thread locker color indicates wrong strength/threadlocking compound, deviates from OEM/engineering specs. Therefore decision fail. Risk: medium or high? Thread locker color red vs blue: red is high strength typically requires heat to remove, blue is medium strength removable. Misapplication can affect assembly integrity, serviceability, vibration loosening. If spec requires red and blue applied, it's a defect. Risk maybe medium. But need decide. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete ~90? roughed in. Value 0. Confidence 0.95. event_time, rate, sound_source default empty/null? Schema has types: sound_source string, event_time number, rate number. Use empty string and 0. order empty list, workable empty list. findings: thread locker color misapplied (blue instead of red per ID/spec), does not meet engineering/OEM spec. actions: remove/clean misapplied thread locker, apply correct thread locker per spec, re-torque to spec, verify with QA. rationale brief. references: OEM/engineering specs, thread locker manufacturer application guidelines (e.g., Loctite color-code). Need ensure JSON valid and no extra whitespace? fine.

Check schema: "work_stage": rough-complete. "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"]. "percent_complete": 95 maybe. "value": 0. "sound_source": "". "confidence": 0.95. "event_time": 0.0. "rate":0.0. "order": []. "workable": []. "findings": ["Thread locker color misapplied (blue vs. red - ID)"] etc. "actions": ["Remove and clean misapplied thread locker", "Apply correct thread locker per OEM/engineering spec", "Re-torque fastener to specified torque", "Inspect/verify at quality gate"]. "rationale": "The specified thread locker color was not used..." references: ["OEM/engineering specs"].

Should risk be high? Since thread locker critical to fastening, maybe medium. But if red required and blue applied, joint may loosen under vibration; could be high in critical assembly. However prompt asks decide. We'll choose medium. But we might want high if failure could cause safety-critical. Manufacturing floor standard work, quality gate. Misapplied thread locker is noncompliant but observed before closeout. Risk medium. Could be high depending on application but no specifics. Use medium.

Need ensure JSON output raw. We'll output.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "medium",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": [
    "installed-defective",
    "non-compliant"
  ],
  "percent_complete": 90,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Thread locker color misapplied (blue vs. red - ID); does not match the OEM/engineering specification for this fastener.",
    "Incorrect thread locker can compromise clamp load retention, vibration resistance, and future serviceability."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop closure of this assembly at the quality gate.",
    "Remove and clean the misapplied thread locker from the affected fastener(s) per manufacturer instructions.",
    "Apply the specified thread locker color/strength to the fastener(s).",
    "Re-torque to the OEM/engineering torque specification and verify at the quality gate."
  ],
  "rationale": "The installed thread locker color does not match the specified color (blue applied where red is required), so the fastening does not comply with OEM/engineering specs and is defective as installed.",
  "references": [
    "OEM/engineering specs",
    "Thread-locking adhesive manufacturer color-code/application guidelines"
  ]
}
